openmeter/watermill/driver/noop.purpose: Zero-value no-op implementation of message.Publisher for disabled-feature Wire paths (e.g. credits.enabled=false). Satisfies the compiler with Publish and Close returning nil; no messages are ever sent.

patterns.name: Compile-time interface assertion
patterns.description: A package-level var ensures the struct always satisfies the Watermill message.Publisher interface, catching drift at compile time.

patterns.name: Zero-value struct with no constructor
patterns.description: noop.Publisher has no fields; instantiate directly with &noop.Publisher{}. No constructor is needed or should be added.

patterns.name: Both methods return nil
patterns.description: Publish and Close must always return nil — callers treat non-nil errors as real failures even when the feature is disabled.
patterns.example: func (Publisher) Publish(topic string, messages ...*message.Message) error { return nil }

key_file_guides.role: Entire package: the Publisher struct, the compile-time interface assertion, and no-op Publish/Close methods.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Both Publish and Close must return nil. Do not add fields, dependencies, logging, or metrics — this defeats the purpose of a disabled-feature noop.

anti_patterns: Adding logging or metrics to Publisher — adds side-effects in disabled paths.
Returning non-nil errors from Publish or Close — callers treat errors as real failures.
Adding constructor functions that accept dependencies — the zero-value struct is the intended usage.
Growing this package beyond the single Publisher type — other noop concerns belong in their own packages.
Using this publisher in non-disabled-feature paths — only use it in Wire providers gated by a feature flag.

decisions.rationale: Maximally simple; Wire providers return &noop.Publisher{} inline with no setup, making disabled-feature paths trivially safe and dependency-free.

decisions.decision: Package scoped to a single type.
decisions.rationale: One type (Publisher) keeps the package trivially auditable and prevents it from accumulating hidden side-effects.

code_examples.scenario: Wire provider returning a noop publisher when a feature is disabled
